seoTitle: Olympic Dam vs One Tree Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Olympic Dam and One Tree Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Olympic Dam vs One Tree Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Olympic Dam and One Tree Hill.
heading: Olympic Dam vs One Tree Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Olympic Dam, South Australia and One Tree Hill, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: One Tree Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Olympic Dam (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Olympic Dam or One Tree Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Olympic Dam has a flood risk score of 37/100, while One Tree Hill scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Olympic Dam scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. One Tree Hill scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Olympic Dam or One Tree Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. One Tree Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
